stemHtml: A light twin engined aircraft departs La Fleche aerodrome (LFAL) in France to route across to Nantes Airport (LFRS). Due to weather conditions the aircraft receives clearance to cross Nantes control zone, Class D airspace, IFR at 2000 ft with the pilot's own routing. Which of the following is correct regarding the responsibility for terrain separation?

options.a: The Air Traffic Controller is responsible for terrain separation when giving the aircraft an altitude to fly under IFR.
options.b: The Air Traffic Controller is responsible for terrain separation for all IFR flights in Class A, B, C or D airspace.
options.c: The Pilot-in-Command is always responsible for terrain separation.
options.d: The Pilot-in-Command is always responsible for terrain separation, except when following vectors under IFR.
